mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 7.6 at decision point, approaching threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls signals a broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue growth expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over 60-day windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, a sustained rise in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and margin compression, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during late-cycle phases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly tracks corporate profit growth, creating a persistent positive correlation with equity returns. Rising GDPC1 consistently predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.20, or forward returns are negative in more than 60% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations trigger central bank tightening fears, increasing the equity risk premium and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.831 implies elevated inflation pressures
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and employment indicate accelerating consumer demand, which rapidly translates into near-term earnings upgrades and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-PAYEMS signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing recovery phase, monthly housing starts exceeding 900 thousand units signal improving household formation and construction sector momentum, which lifts related equity sectors and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of months where HOUST surpasses 900 during a housing recovery, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 3-year backtest.
